title: puya sanctae-crucis
intro: Puya sanctae-crucis, also known as the Andean potato bush, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The leaves and stems of Puya sanctae-crucis can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
